title: Sticherus trifurcans
intro: Sticherus trifurcans, also known as the forked rachis fern or forked rachis tree fern, is a type of tree fern native to tropical Asia, characterized by its distinctive forked rachis and delicate fronds. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Sticherus trifurcans include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infused in hot water to make a tea, and it is also used in the production of herbal capsules, tablets, and tinctures.
taxonomy: Sticherus trifurcans is classified as a species of fern in the family Gleicheniaceae. This family is a part of the order Gleicheniales, which belongs to the division Pteridophyta. The Pteridophyta division contains vascular plants that reproduce via spores and are characterized by their fern-like appearance. Sticherus trifurcans is native to tropical regions and is a relatively small fern species. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.
